title: gastrolobium ilicifolium var. lobatum
intro: Gastrolobium ilicifolium var. lobatum, also known as poison-pea or poison bean, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, native to Western Australia. The plant contains toxic alkaloids, which provide its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and antifungal effects. The medicinal properties of Gastrolobium ilicifolium var. lobatum offer potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. However, due to its toxicity, the plant is rarely used in herbal preparations and is typically handled with caution by trained professionals.
